name: windows-everything-productionization
title: Productionize Windows Everything Search
description: Close SDK/CLI strategy, packaged native availability, diagnostics, Windows acceptance, and P50/P95 evidence for Everything search.
creator: TalexDreamSoul
notes: Implementation, diagnostics, managed SDK resources, hosted Windows SDK/CLI/unavailable evidence, focused tests, and Windows package/native gates are complete; packaged CoreBox UI acceptance remains open.

meta.nextAction: On an interactive Windows packaged app, capture normal/@file/structured-filter CoreBox result/empty/degraded evidence, attach it to the Windows acceptance manifest, and run strict manifest verification.
meta.blocker: The hosted backend/package gate does not exercise interactive packaged CoreBox search modes; no automated packaged UI probe currently produces the required screenshots and acceptance manifest fields.
meta.evidence: GitHub Actions run 29628880312 passed all steps. Artifact windows-everything-production-evidence contains SDK 1.4.1.1032 with 200/200 non-empty samples and P50/P95/max 2/3/4ms; CLI 1.1.0.30 with 200/200 non-empty samples and P50/P95/max 8/9/26ms; exact unavailable state SDK error 2 and CLI exit 8; packaged everything.js, everything-resources.js, and 185856-byte tuff_native_everything.node SHA-256 3fe24155107a74ed61521f4c943f23050ea17c9fc17873ea144fe0791a576d83. Local verification also passed 9 native tests, 161 focused tests, node/web typechecks, production build, lint, PowerShell parser, actionlint, Trellis validation, and reviewer re-check.
